title: Apple M7 Ultra targets 1.5 TB of unified memory and Blackwell-class performance
summary: Rumors suggest that Apple would skip the M6 chips to focus on the M7 with massive AI and memory improvements, although its launch would depend on memory availability.

title: AMD prepares 8x multi-frame generation in its drivers to compete with NVIDIA
summary: AMD's experimental drivers hint at the next evolution of FSR with up to 8x frame multiplication.

title: Valve will fix the overheating warning on Steam Machines with a BIOS update
summary: The red warning light was activating prematurely; the BIOS will raise the threshold to 100 °C to avoid false positives.

title: Meta removes AI feature that generated images from Instagram photos after criticism
summary: The feature, which used users' images without permission, generated strong backlash and was disabled.

title: Lorde criticizes Ray-Ban Meta AI glasses at a festival in Madrid and calls them 'unsexy'
summary: The New Zealand singer expressed her rejection of Meta's wearable technology during her performance at the festival.

title: Anthropic extends free access to Claude Fable 5 for subscribers amid price war with OpenAI
summary: The extension allows users to try the AI model until July 19 while OpenAI launches GPT-5.6 Sol.

title: Data centers in Ireland consumed almost as much electricity as all households in the country in 2025
summary: Servers accounted for 23% of national energy consumption despite grid restrictions.

title: Uber-Waymo conflict intensifies with lobbying and driver protests in Atlanta
summary: Uber pressures regulators while drivers call for limits on Waymo's expansion, and TechCrunch publishes an ultimatum on robotaxis.

title: Leak of police drone images in San Francisco and end of LAPD agreement with license plate reader cameras
summary: Urban surveillance with drones and cameras faces growing public scrutiny and decisions to restrict its use.

title: Concern over use of AI to generate fake journalistic content that replaces human journalists
summary: A journalist was fired but the company continued publishing AI-generated articles under his name, and a study points to LinkedIn as the biggest distributor of AI-generated content.

title: Secure Boot updates cause issues in Windows 11 and Debian 13.6
summary: Microsoft acknowledges failures in the Secure Boot update on Windows 11 while Debian releases version 13.6 with security changes.

title: Claude Code incorporates built-in browser and optimizes token sending
summary: Anthropic's AI tool can now read and click on websites, while a comparison shows it sends fewer tokens than OpenCode.

title: Windows 11 will integrate your phone and its Copilot assistant consumes 1 GB of RAM
summary: Microsoft tests deeper integration with phones and Copilot now reports on PC performance while using a significant amount of memory.

title: German consortium launches open model Soofi S and AI buyers opt for cheaper systems
summary: Soofi S outperforms other models in English and German, while the market shifts toward more affordable AI solutions.

title: The ability to discover new information is in decline, according to critics
summary: Two articles warn about the loss of ability to find relevant content and question current algorithms.

title: AI replaces nurses in New York and causes a farewell to Photoshop
summary: Automation is affecting creative and healthcare professions, raising concerns about care quality.

title: Debate over legal responsibility for crimes committed by AI
summary: Experts discuss who should take the blame when an autonomous system commits a crime.

title: Lenovo launches a Lunar Lake mini PC with non-expandable memory and a Legion 7a gaming laptop with RTX 5070
summary: The company presents devices for different segments with striking specifications.

title: PS5 emulator already loads 3D games and Dreamcast receives support on Linux
summary: The emulation scene advances with the PS5 emulator reaching GTA V menus and the inclusion of Dreamcast in Linux.

title: Shortage of copper and fiberglass threatens industry, while Huawei builds its own DRAM factory
summary: Critical materials for electronics are scarce and manufacturers seek alternatives like in-house memory production.
